fund.name: Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund - Direct Plan
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Low-cost index fund tracking Nifty Midcap 150. Midcaps have delivered strong long-term returns and suit your moderate risk appetite and 38-year horizon. Expense ratio is just 0.2%, keeping more of your money working for you.

fund.name: ICICI Prudential Equity & Debt Fund - Direct Plan - Growth
fund.fund_house: ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Hybrid fund with ~70% in stocks and 30% in bonds. Provides stability while staying equity-taxed (favourable for long-term gains). Helps balance risk as you start investing. A well-established fund with consistent performance.

plan.setup_phase:
Step 1: Download Groww or Kuvera app.
Step 2: Complete KYC using your PAN and Aadhaar.
Step 3: Open a direct mutual fund account.
Step 4: Start a monthly SIP of ₹5,000 in Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund.
Step 5: Invest your ₹20,000 lumpsum in ICICI Prudential Equity & Debt Fund.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Check if SIPs are running smoothly.
After 1 year: Review fund performance vs category average.
Yearly: Rebalance if allocation drifts by more than 5%.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Once a year, check your portfolio split. If one fund has grown to more than 75% or less than 65% of your total value, adjust by moving money to keep it at 70% stocks (index fund) and 30% hybrid (balanced fund).
plan.rationale: At 22, you have 38 years to grow your money — time is your biggest advantage. We’re using a simple, low-cost mix: 70% in a midcap index fund for growth, and 30% in a balanced hybrid fund for stability. This fits your moderate risk level and keeps things simple. The ₹5,000/month SIP will grow with step-ups, and the ₹20,000 kickstarts compounding early.
risks: Midcap stocks can fall 30-40% in a bad year — your portfolio may drop temporarily. This is normal.
Hybrid funds reduce risk but can still go down if markets fall.
Inflation may reduce purchasing power if returns don’t beat it long-term.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase SIP by 10% every year to match expected salary growth

allocation_schedule.trigger: After 10 years, slightly reduce equity exposure
After 25 years, shift gradually to safer assets

aps.reasoning: The plan has 70% allocation to a passive midcap index fund (pure passive equity) and 30% to an actively managed hybrid fund. This gives passive_instrument_fraction = 0.7. Turnover is low with annual rebalancing only if allocations drift significantly (score 0.8). Cost emphasis is moderate - explicitly mentioned low expense ratio for index fund but not for hybrid fund (score 0.6). Research focus is minimal - relies on index funds and established hybrid fund without deep analysis (score 0.8). Time horizon is very long-term oriented with 38-year perspective and gradual allocation shifts (score 0.9). Portfolio activeness: 70% passive index fund, 30% active hybrid fund - overall moderately passive (score 0.7).

pqs.reasoning: The plan shows good alignment with Remy's goals: simple 2-fund approach matches his preference for minimal complexity, midcap focus aligns with long horizon and moderate risk appetite. Diversification is limited - only 2 funds from same market (India) with midcap concentration risk. Risk-return appropriateness is reasonable but projecting 18.7% returns may be optimistic. Internally consistent with clear rationale, allocation matches stated strategy. Tax efficiency is good for new_regime investor - both funds are equity-taxed (midcap index and aggressive hybrid) which is appropriate. However, the hybrid fund's 1% expense ratio is high compared to the index fund's 0.2%.
